title: SB 1254 (The FRESH Act) connects incarcerated individuals with vital services such as CalFresh and other resources prior to release in order to increase the success of reentry.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: The FRESH Act allows incarcerated individuals to pre-apply for CalFresh benefits 90 days prior to their release.
Expanding eligibility for CalFresh would unlock an estimated $1.8 billion in additional CalFresh dollars from the federal government each year.
The new federal money would support local grocery stores and farms.
The FRESH Act aims to reduce food insecurity in California.
1 in 5 formerly incarcerated people suffered from food insecurity in 2019.
The FRESH Act creates a uniform process for reentry for all incarcerated individuals.
Expanding access to benefits and programs increases the success of reentry.
The new federal funding could generate an estimated additional $3.3 billion in economic activity per year.
